statute: Michigan Occupational Code MCL § 339.2401 et seq. (Residential Builders); MCL § 339.2601 (Maintenance and Alteration Contractors)
license_label: Michigan Residential Builders or Maintenance and Alteration Contractors License Number
license_notes: Michigan requires state license for residential work. Residential Builders License for new home construction. Maintenance and Alteration Contractors License for remodeling/repairs. Unlicensed = no lien rights on residential projects. Penalty: $5,000-$25,000 fine, up to 2-4 years in prison.

mandatory_clauses.label: Michigan contractor license disclosure
Insurance disclosure
mandatory_clauses.text:
General Liability Insurance: {{gl_status}} — Provider: {{gl_provider}}
Workers Compensation: {{wc_status}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Right to cancel (home solicitation)
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL (HOME SOLICITATION): If this contract was solicited at your home, you have the right to cancel within three business days. To cancel, mail or deliver written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Misdemeanor: $5,000-$25,000 fine + 1-2 years prison. Death/injury while unlicensed = up to 4 years. No lien rights for unlicensed residential contractors.
notes: Michigan requires separate licenses for residential builders (new construction) and maintenance/alteration contractors (remodeling). Commercial contractors check with local municipality. Unlicensed contractors on residential projects have no mechanics lien rights. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements (MI waives state notice for contracts at seller's office).
